note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Finite.lean:321 -- def IsRegularOfDegree (d : ℕ) : Prop := ∀ v : V, G.degree v = d, with docstring 'A locally finite simple graph is regular of degree d if every vertex has degree d' -- this IS the standard definition of a regular graph.

Mathlib/Analysis/Complex/Exponential.lean:80 -- nonrec def exp (x : ℝ) : ℝ (in namespace Real). Mathlib has no standalone named constant for e; the constant is represented throughout as the term `Real.exp 1` (see e.g. Real.exp_one_lt_d9/Real.exp_one_gt_d9 bounds in Mathlib/Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Exp.lean), so the closest citable decl is the general exponential function of which e is the value at 1 -- a generalization, not an exact def-of-the-constant.

Mathlib/Computability (verified as a top-level module key in catalog/data/hierarchy.json, with subfolders Primrec, Partrec, TuringMachine, Halting, Reduce, MyhillNerode, etc.) is Mathlib's formal home for computability theory -- no single decl captures the whole field, so a folder-level container is the honest join.
